creator: hsmtkk@gmail.com
text:
Edwin <edwin@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
> Subject: Re: ports/148775: textproc/mxml and textproc/mini-xml/ are the s=
ame ports
> =C2=A0Maintainer of textproc/mxml,
> =C2=A0Please note that PR ports/148775 has just been submitted.
> =C2=A0If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> =C2=A0you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patc=
h
> =C2=A0and a committer will take care of it.
> =C2=A0The full text of the PR can be found at:
> =C2=A0 =C2=A0 http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3Dports/148775

Bapt <baptiste.daroussin@gmail.com> wrote:
> textproc/mxml and textproc/mini-xml are the same ports
> the first one is uptodate the last one is outdated.
> I thinks one should be removed

This is true.
textproc/mxml and textproc/mini-xml are the same software.

The software is provided here
http://www.minixml.org/
and the current stable version is Mini-XML 2.6.

textproc/mxml is under version 2.6,
so upgrade is not required.

textproc/mini-xml is under version 2.2.2_1 (outdated).
The Mini-XML does not support this version.
I think FreeBSD project should remove textproc/mini-xml from the ports.
But, I am not a maintainer of this port.

--=20
Kouki Hashimoto
hsmtkk@gmail.com
